prerequisites: An active Firebolt account. Sign up at firebolt.io.
A Firebolt database and an engine to run queries against, created beforehand (the destination needs both the database and engine names).

A Firebolt service account with its associated user, so you can obtain a client ID and client secret for programmatic access. Creating and managing service accounts requires the organization admin role.
Your Firebolt account name (the account within your organization that holds the target database and engine).

If you use the S3 loading method: an AWS S3 bucket in the same region as your Firebolt database, plus AWS credentials (access key ID and secret access key) that have read, write, and delete permissions on that staging bucket.

authMethods.name: Service account (client ID + client secret)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Firebolt service account. Provide the service account's ID as client_id and its generated secret as client_secret. Firebolt exchanges these credentials for an access token used on subsequent requests.

authMethods.name: AWS S3 credentials (S3 loading method)
authMethods.summary: When loading_method is set to S3 (external table), additionally supply AWS aws_key_id and aws_key_secret for a user or key pair with read, write, and delete access to the staging bucket. These are only needed for the S3 loading path, not for the SQL Insert path.

setupSteps.title: Create a Firebolt database and engine
setupSteps.body: In Firebolt, create the target database and an engine to execute queries. Note the database name and engine name; both are required by the connector.

setupSteps.title: Create a service account and get its client ID
setupSteps.body: As an organization admin, go to Configure -> Service accounts -> Create a service account (or run CREATE SERVICE ACCOUNT in SQL), giving it a name and associating it with a user. Then open the Service accounts table and copy the value from the ID column. Use this as client_id.

setupSteps.title: Generate the service account secret
setupSteps.body: From Configure -> Service accounts, click the three dots next to your service account and choose Rotate secret (or run CALL fb_GENERATESERVICEACCOUNTKEY('service_account_name') in SQL). Copy the secret immediately, since Firebolt does not store it and it cannot be retrieved later. Use this as client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Enter account, database, and engine
setupSteps.body: Provide your Firebolt account name (account), and the database and engine names created earlier. Leave host blank for most use cases; only set it if you need to point at a specific Firebolt backend URL.

setupSteps.title: Choose a loading method
setupSteps.body: Select SQL for direct INSERT queries via the Firebolt SDK (not recommended for production as it does not scale well), or S3 to stage data through an external table. For S3, supply the bucket name, the bucket region (same region as your Firebolt database), and AWS credentials (aws_key_id and aws_key_secret) with read, write, and delete permissions on the staging bucket.

sources.title: Manage programmatic access (service accounts) - Firebolt Documentation
